subject: English / First Flight
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the story of Madam and her desire for independence
Identify key events during Madam’s bus ride
Develop reading comprehension and summarization skills
Learn about self-confidence, courage, and curiosity

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Madam and Context
topics_covered: Who Madam is and her personality
Setting and context of the story
Madam’s desire to explore the city independently
student_activities: Discuss what kind of person Madam is
Predict what might happen during her journey
assessment: Oral Q&A on Madam and her personality
Short written note on Madam’s desire to explore

lesson_plan.title: Madam’s Bus Journey Begins
topics_covered: Interactions with the conductor and passengers
teaching_strategy: Read passages describing the start of the journey
Use simple discussion to highlight Madam’s feelings
student_activities: List Madam’s observations during the journey
Discuss how Madam feels at different moments
assessment: Oral Q&A on beginning of the journey
Short written note on Madam’s initial reactions

lesson_plan.title: Madam’s Experiences on the Bus
topics_covered: Different experiences and surprises during the ride
teaching_strategy: Use images or illustrations to support comprehension
student_activities: Identify incidents Madam finds surprising or funny
Discuss what shows her courage and independence
assessment: Short written note on one interesting incident

lesson_plan.title: Lessons from Madam’s Ride
teaching_strategy: Discuss moral lessons in simple terms
Encourage students to reflect on their own experiences
student_activities: Discuss in pairs the lessons learned from Madam
Write one sentence on what they can learn from Madam’s actions
assessment: Oral discussion on lessons and reflections
Short written note connecting story to personal experience

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Reflection
topics_covered: Summary of Madam’s journey and experiences
Personal reflections on courage, curiosity, and independence
teaching_strategy: Use timeline or flashcards for revision
Encourage students to share favorite incidents or lessons
student_activities: Create a simple timeline of Madam’s journey
Write a few lines on what they liked most about Madam
assessment: Oral quiz on key events and lessons
Class discussion on reflections and personal insights

teaching_aids: Map or diagram of bus route (illustrative)
Images of buses and city scenes
Flashcards with key incidents and lessons
teacher_tip: Use storytelling and visuals to engage students. Focus on Madam’s journey, experiences, and moral lessons. Encourage students to share their own experiences of curiosity and courage.
